content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 7, 1994

Gail Martini
Tri-County Apartment Assn.
792 Meridian Way, Suite A
San Jose, CA 95126

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-94-78

Dear Ms. Martini:

This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that if the
Association intends to hold a joint fundraiser for both Tri-PAC
and Tri-Issues PAC, the Association should advise contributors of
the amount designated for each committee. If the Association
raises funds for one of the committees and later transfers funds
to the other, it is not necessary to notify past contributors of
the transfer as long as it was not the intent of the Association
at the time of raising the funds that some portion would be
earmarked for the other committee.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: When holding a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, must an organization notify contributors about fund designations and transfers between committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If holding a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, contributors must be advised of amounts designated for each committee. However, if funds raised for one committee are later transferred to another without prior intent to earmark for the other committee, past contributors need not be notified of the transfer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter documenting prior telephone advice rather than a formal advice letter with separate Q/A sections. No verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist; advice is embedded in body text. Document cites Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: When holding a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, must an organization notify contributors about fund designations and transfers between committees?

CONCLUSION: If holding a joint fundraiser for multiple PACs, contributors must be advised of amounts designated for each committee. However, if funds raised for one committee are later transferred to another without prior intent to earmark for the other committee, past contributors need not be notified of the transfer.

(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that if the Association intends to hold a joint fundraiser for both Tri-PAC and Tri-Issues PAC, the Association should advise contributors of the amount designated for each committee. If the Association raises funds for one of the committees and later transfers funds to the other, it is not necessary to notify past contributors of the transfer as long as it was not the intent of the Association at the time of raising the funds that some portion would be earmarked for the other committee. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel By: Colleen McGee Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Tri-County Apartment Association regarding campaign disclosure requirements for joint fundraisers involving multiple PACs, specifically addressing contributor notification requirements for fund designations and inter-committee transfers.
